scope: FQ-09S: make the existing skill tree feel like a Skyrim-style star map through an 8-16bit Coheronia aesthetic, without adding new perks, currencies, or progression rules.
summary: scripts/ui/skill_tree_panel.gd restyled in place: a deep night-sky PanelContainer StyleBox; the node canvas hooks the CanvasItem draw signal and renders a fixed-seed 110-pixel starfield, one faint constellation line per prerequisite pair (LINK_OWNED green a0.55 when both ends purchased, LINK_OPEN white a0.35 toward an available node, LINK_LOCKED a0.18 otherwise), and a pixel star glyph above each node (4-arm crosshair; owned nodes get a larger 8-arm star). Buttons wear one shared StyleBoxFlat per state (dark plaque, state-colored 1px border, state-colored text; intentionally shared and commented) replacing whole-button modulate. Everything mechanical is untouched: perks.json, point economy, prerequisite/cost gates, save ownership, K/Esc/click wiring, purchase_requested -> game_root.try_purchase_perk, [OWNED]/[LOCKED] markers, and the inspector text format (_refresh_inspector unmodified, byte-identical output). Cosmetic wording only: title "SKILL CONSTELLATIONS — MINER LANE", "Planned constellations: ..."; CANVAS_MARGIN.y 16 -> 26 gives row-0 glyphs headroom.
acceptance: Panel reads as a star/constellation tree at 8-16bit scale: night sky, deterministic pixel starfield, constellation links, star glyphs, state-colored plaques
Existing smoke checks for purchase, persistence, state, and inspection pass unchanged: all six fq06_* checks green against the restyled panel

Added visual state is covered by a hook: link_count() equals the live lane's prerequisite-pair count derived from the same perk_lanes() data (fq09s_constellation_links_match_prereqs: links=2 expected=2, non-vacuous)
No new live mechanics, perk effects, or progression economy
Smoke 185/185 PASS at 2026-07-09T15:53:54 (re-run green after review nits) on waited windowed Godot 4.6.1; validator PASS; capsule doctor healthy; git diff --check clean
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 185/185 PASS incl. new fq09s check and all fq06_* checks unchanged
decisions: Restyle happens entirely inside the existing panel class — same external interface (setup/refresh/select_node/info_text/purchase_requested), so hud, game_root, and smoke integration needed zero changes
The canvas draws via the CanvasItem draw signal on the existing Control — no new scene, shader, or animation framework

Link and glyph colors derive from the same perk_state values the buttons use, and the fq09s smoke check derives its expected link count from the same perk_lanes() data — presentation cannot invent or drop an edge
Inspector text format deliberately untouched (byte-identical) because fq06_panel_opens_and_inspects matches substrings of it
Button text keeps the [OWNED]/[LOCKED] marker format; readability at 16px favors text markers over icon-only states

Swarm pipeline: 1 haiku recon scout (panel map + smoke constraint list), one implementation lane (orchestrator), sonnet diff review -> SIGNABLE, 3 nits applied (unused loop var, shared-StyleBox comment, HANDOFF glyph wording), full loop re-ran green
risks: README screenshot 05_skill_tree.png still shows the pre-FQ-09S styling; regenerate via the COHERONIA_SHOTS tour when convenient
Shared per-state StyleBoxFlat instances must be cloned before any future per-button mutation (commented in code)
Starfield/links are code-drawn; FQ-09V/FQ-09A can later route UI art through the asset pipeline without touching mechanics
